name: TV Control
description: Control Android TVs paired to OpenEnsemble — launch apps, control playback and volume, search, show a message or photo on screen, view Home Assistant cameras, check what's playing, and manage the TV video library's sources (local folders and SMB network shares).
intent_patterns_note: Only consumed by the specialist router (chat-dispatch/llm-loop.mjs) for manifests with service:true — dormant while this skill is service:false. Every pattern must require an explicit TV mention: a bare volume/playback phrase here would hijack speaker-volume commands the moment service is flipped on.
intent_examples: go back to the previous scene
turn the volume down on the tv
search for the office on netflix
show a message on the tv
put this photo on the tv screen
show me the front door camera
show me who's at the door on the tv
what's playing on the tv right now
what's on the living room tv
what app is the tv on
what video folders can the tv see
add a video source for the tv
add the nas share to the tv videos
save this file to the videos folder
put that movie on the nas share
systemPromptAddition:
## TV Control

Use the `tv_*` tools to control Android TVs paired to OpenEnsemble — launching apps, media playback, volume, search, showing text or photos on screen, viewing Home Assistant cameras, and checking what's playing. If {{USER_NAME}} is speaking through a TV itself, that TV is used automatically; only pass `device` when they name a specific TV ("the bedroom TV") or a tool tells you multiple TVs are online and asks you to pick one. Call the tool directly for clear commands ("pause the TV", "put on Netflix", "show me the front door") — don't ask for confirmation first. If a tool reports an error (TV offline, app not found, ambiguous camera name), relay it in one short sentence, and when it lists options (installed apps, camera names, TV names) read those back so {{USER_NAME}} can pick.

The TV's video library is served from named video sources (local folders on the OE server, or SMB network shares like //nas/media). Anyone can list them with `tv_video_sources_list`. Adding or removing sources (`tv_video_source_add` / `tv_video_source_remove`) and saving a server-local file into one (`tv_videos_save`) are admin-only — the tools check this themselves. If a video-source tool refuses or reports an error, relay its message verbatim: it often includes the exact fix (e.g. an install command).

function.name: tv_launch_app
function.description: Launch an app on an Android TV (e.g. Netflix, YouTube, Hulu, Disney+, Plex, Prime Video). The device resolves the spoken app name to an installed app.

app.description: Spoken/display name of the app to launch, e.g. 'Netflix' or 'YouTube'.

device.description: Optional TV name to target when more than one TV is online (case-insensitive substring match, e.g. 'living room'). Not needed if the user is speaking to the TV directly.

function.name: tv_media_control
function.description: Send a media transport command (play/pause/stop/skip/seek) to whatever is currently playing on an Android TV.

key.description: The media transport action to perform.

device.description: Optional TV name to target when more than one TV is online (case-insensitive substring match). Not needed if the user is speaking to the TV directly.

function.name: tv_set_volume
function.description: Change the volume on an Android TV. Provide exactly ONE of pct, delta, or mute.

pct.description: Set volume to an absolute percentage, 0-100.

delta.description: Adjust volume relative to its current level, e.g. 10 for up, -10 for down.

mute.description: true to mute, false to unmute.

device.description: Optional TV name to target when more than one TV is online (case-insensitive substring match). Not needed if the user is speaking to the TV directly.

function.name: tv_search
function.description: Search for content on an Android TV, optionally scoped to a specific app.

query.description: What to search for, e.g. 'alien' or 'the office'.

app.description: Optional app to search within, e.g. 'YouTube' or 'Netflix'. Leave unset to let the device pick.

device.description: Optional TV name to target when more than one TV is online (case-insensitive substring match). Not needed if the user is speaking to the TV directly.

function.name: tv_show_on_tv
function.description: Show a short text message and/or a photo on an Android TV screen for a few seconds (wakes the screen first if needed). Provide at least one of title, body, or image_url.

body.description: Longer body text, shown under the title.

image_url.description: URL (may be OE-server-relative, e.g. '/api/...') of an image to display. When set, the card renders as an image with title/body as caption.

duration_seconds.description: How long to keep it on screen, in seconds. Defaults to 15; clamped to 3-120.

device.description: Optional TV name to target when more than one TV is online (case-insensitive substring match). Not needed if the user is speaking to the TV directly.

function.name: tv_show_camera
function.description: Show a Home Assistant camera's live snapshot on an Android TV — e.g. 'show me the front door'. Resolves the camera by entity_id (e.g. 'camera.front_door') or by fuzzy match on its friendly name.

camera.description: Camera entity_id (e.g. 'camera.front_door') or a friendly-name phrase (e.g. 'front door', 'driveway', 'garage').

device.description: Optional TV name to target when more than one TV is online (case-insensitive substring match). Not needed if the user is speaking to the TV directly.

function.name: tv_now_playing
function.description: Check what's currently on an Android TV — the foreground app and any now-playing media.

device.description: Optional TV name to target when more than one TV is online (case-insensitive substring match). Not needed if the user is speaking to the TV directly.

function.name: tv_video_sources_list
function.description: List the TV video library's sources — each source's name, type (local folder or SMB network share), and whether it's currently available (with the reason when it isn't).

function.name: tv_video_source_add
function.description: Add a source to the TV video library (admin only). Accepts either a local folder path on the OE server (e.g. '/srv/media/Movies') or a guest-accessible SMB network share (e.g. '//nas/media').

name.description: Display name for the source (1-32 characters: letters, digits, spaces, underscores, hyphens), e.g. 'Movies' or 'NAS'.

share_or_path.description: Absolute folder path on the OE server (e.g. '/srv/media/Movies') or an SMB share written as //host/share (e.g. '//nas/media').

subfolder.description: Optional subfolder inside the path/share to use as the source's root, e.g. 'movies/kids'.

port.description: Optional SMB port when the share isn't on the default 445.

function.name: tv_video_source_remove
function.description: Remove a source from the TV video library by name (admin only). The folder or share's files themselves are not deleted.

name.description: Name of the video source to remove, as shown by tv_video_sources_list.

function.name: tv_videos_save
function.description: Save a file that is already on the OE server into a TV video-library source (admin only) — e.g. copy a downloaded or generated video into the Videos folder or onto a NAS share so it shows up on the TV.

file.description: Absolute path of the file on the OE server, e.g. '/srv/media/incoming/clip.mp4'.

source.description: Name of the video source to save into, as shown by tv_video_sources_list.

subfolder.description: Optional subfolder inside the source to place the file in (created if missing), e.g. 'home-movies'.
